rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al VS. GCS, INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 15, 2011, Line 6, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions And Request For Production Of Documents. Hearing required unless the parties have resolved all issues raised by the motion or they have agreed to continue the hearing to another date. If the parties have agreed to continue the hearing to another date, they should so inform the court by an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 15, 2011, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GLORIA TRAN's Motion To Tax Costs. Granted as to the claimed expert costs and denied as to the claimed deposition costs. The expert costs are not recoverable per Code of Civil Procedure 1033.5(b)(1). The deposition costs were reasonably incurred and reasonable in amount and recoverable per 1033.5(a)(3) and/or 1033.5(c)(4). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL A. PALMA VS. PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 15, 2011, Line 8, DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff again fails to allege that the defendant violated a specific mandatory duty. A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant¿s exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also, once again, fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. rulings.Case Title: WENDY DUJARDIN VS. RODAN AND FIELDS, LLC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $14,700
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 15, 2011, Line 3, PLAINTIFF WENDY DUJARDIN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $14,700. Hearing required. The parties are directed to engage in further meet and confer in an effort to resolve or narrow all the issues raised by the motion and report on their progress in doing so at the hearing. In the event that both parties agree that it would be useful for the hearing to be continued and both parties agree to the continued hearing date, the parties should send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org stating their agreement to continue the hearing to a specific date. As guidance for the parties, the court¿s preliminary view is that many, if not most, of the objections lack merit and that plaintiff is entitled to receive no later than January 31, 2012 many, if not most or all, responsive documents and ESI subject to simple standard-language protective order except for privileged documents and ESI, but as to which a privilege log should be provided. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS A. SMITH et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Permit Transfer Of The Property And For Attys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 15, 2011. Line 20.PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS SMITH'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Permit Transfer Of The Property And For Attorneys Fees. Granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs state the seven items of relief they request on page 9 of their memorandum filed with their moving papers. Plaintiffs are entitled to an expungement of the lis pendens (item 5) since defendant 300 Parking Street LLC states that it no longer is the owner of the disputed note and that it has removed the lis pendens. All other items of relief sought by plaintiffs are denied. Plaintiffs provide no authority that this court is permitted to grant items 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 absent a trial, a recognized dispositive motion, an order to show cause or a preliminary injunction motion and, even if the court could grant such relief, plaintiffs have not provided any admissible evidence to support the relief. The declaration of Mr. Bonis, while it states that is made on personal knowledge, quite obviously is not ¿ at least as to the purported facts as to the asserted illegality of the disputed note. Plaintiffs are not able to recover their frees for this motion (item 7), even the portion of those fees relating to the lis pendens since plaintiffs have provided no evidence of the amount of those fees.Plaintiffs¿ memorandum states that a ¿fee declaration¿ is ¿submitted separately, but the court has not received one. Notwithstanding the denial of all but item 5, it seems apparent that something is very wrong with regard to the disputed note and its serial assignments and that the parties and counsel are advised that they must scrupulously refrain from any fraudulent or other illegal conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HYLEN VS. WILLIAM MITCHELL RYDER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 15, 2011. Line 19. Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Continued from December 8, 2011 to provide the parties an opportunity to resolve their differences. The court reiterates its prior tentative ruling: Both parties are entitled to receive a gross payment of $7240.70 every four weeks less credit card charges attributable to them. If the parties cannot agree on whether this amount is employee compensation, a distribution of or a draw against profits, withdrawal of capital or something else, subject to a final characterization at trial, the amounts will be treated as a draw against profits. Hylen is entitled to recover past amounts since the date Ryder listed himself as an employee with Paychex. If, however, Ryder does not return to and remain at work full-time (excepting vacations and short illnesses) by January 1, 2012 the payments cease as of January 1, 2012 or such later date that Ryder ceases to work full-time. Hylen has full authority to choose Satel¿s payroll provider and is Satel¿s representative for that provider. The court finds that the parties had an agreement to pay themselves while each were working full-time for Satel and Hylen unilaterally and without authority breached that agreement to the detriment of Ryder. The court further finds that, due to Ryder¿s recent leave of absence and his listing himself as an employee with Paychex without disclosing this to Hylen, Hylen should be assigned the responsibility to work with Satel¿s payroll provider. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
